chapters.title: Front Matter: Foreword, General Remarks, Chemistry, Selectivity, Voicing and String-Set Charts
chapters.notes: Title/copyright (pp.1-2), Contents (pp.3-5), Charlie Menees appreciation, Foreword and Acknowledgement, General Remarks (mechanics, alternation, barring, similies), Tonal Chemistry, Basic Voicing Chart for C-E-G, String Set Chart (broken/divided/A/B/D sets), Selectivity. Sets the conceptual and notational framework for the entire book.

chapters.title: The Mighty Triads: First/Second Inversion and Root-Position Triad Scales (Major, Harmonic Minor, Melodic Minor)
chapters.notes: Opens at 'THE MIGHTY TRIADS' (line 1359, p.24) and works through 1st-inversion, 2nd-inversion, and root-position triad scales across Major, Harmonic Minor, and Melodic Minor — closed and open voicings — plus triad arpeggios, contrary motion C major, and inversion-with-upper/middle/lower-voice diatonic line studies. Ends at the boundary before Mixed Minors section.

chapters.title: Mixed Minors
chapters.notes: MIXED MINORS heading appears at line 3349 (p.66) and recurs across pp.66-69 — blends harmonic and melodic minor triads through the scale before the Reductions section opens.

chapters.title: Reductions: 3-to-6 and 6-to-3 Triad Reductions (Major, Harmonic Minor, Melodic Minor)
chapters.notes: REDUCTIONS heading at line 3475 (p.70). Systematic reduction studies — '3 to 6 up/down,' '6 to 3 up/down' — across Major, Harmonic Minor, and Melodic Minor. Long section because each direction is worked in all three tonal modes across the full fingerboard range.

chapters.title: Sixths with Upper-Line Motion (Full Range, All Three Modes)
chapters.notes: Begins at 'Sixths opening up to tenths / The visual fingerboard' (line 4782, p.121) and includes Sixths with Upper Line Motion Major/Harmonic Minor/Melodic Minor Full Range and Sixths with Upper Line Major. Bridges into the chromatic material via the 'visual fingerboard' framing.

chapters.title: Chromatic Triads and Chromatic Scales in Intervals
chapters.notes: SIXTHS WITH UPPER LINE / CHROMATICS, CHROMATICS, AND MORE CHROMATICS material introduces the chromatic-triad block. Covers chromatic triads major/minor, 10th-to-3rd and 8-to-1 transforms, and chromatic scales in 2nds/3rds/4ths/5ths/6ths/8ths/10ths/13ths/15ths plus 'Chromatics Expanded.'

chapters.title: Chromatic Triads with Pedal and Alternating Bass
chapters.notes: CHROMATIC TRIADS WITH PEDAL BASS 'G' opens at line 5953 (p.146) and runs through pedal-G full range, root-position with pedal G, and assorted chromatic-triad with-pedal-and-alternating-bass studies. Concludes with general 'Chromatics' material leading into the Super and Sub framework.

chapters.title: Super and Sub Series: First-Inversion Triads (Major, Harmonic Minor, Melodic Minor)
chapters.notes: SUPER AND SUB SERIES introduction (line 6961, p.166) — explains the Super/Sub concept (all chords as centers; voices moved one step up = super, one step down = sub), counter-motion, and worked examples. First-inversion Super/Sub for Upper/Middle/Lower Voice across Major, Harmonic Minor, and Melodic Minor (with the four-string spread for articulated triads).

chapters.title: Super and Sub Series: Second-Inversion Triads (Major, Harmonic Minor, Melodic Minor)
chapters.notes: SUPER AND SUB SERIES — Second Inversion block beginning at line 7765 (p.181). Covers Super/Sub Upper/Middle/Lower Voice variations across Major, Harmonic Minor, and Melodic Minor for second-inversion triads.

chapters.title: Super and Sub Series: Root-Position Triads and Open-Voicing Alternatives
chapters.notes: SUPER AND SUB SERIES About Root Position Triads (line 8342, p.195) — Super/Sub around root-position Upper Voice Super, Upper Voice Sub for Major and Harmonic Minor, then all-voicings Melodic Minor. Continues into Open Voicing Alternatives (line 10853, p.254 boundary). Largest single block because all root-position permutations are worked exhaustively.

chapters.title: Super and Sub Series: Open Voicings, Multi-Voice Motion and Harmonic Variation
chapters.notes: SUPER AND SUB SERIES - Open Voicings MAJOR & MINOR (line 10853, p.254), multi-voice motion examples (Major and Minor), closed-to-open voicing in progressive steps, harmonic minor super/sub series, and Super and Sub Harmonic Variation on Basic Triad Scales.

chapters.title: Visualizing Harmonic Situations / The Magic of the Chromatic Scale, Contrary Motion Chromatic Triads, 5th Finger Principle
chapters.notes: VISUALIZING heading (line 11213, p.263). Covers the magic-of-the-chromatic-scale framing, contrary motion chromatic triads with pedal/alternating bass, the 5th Finger Principle, and the opening of Chromatics/Triads/Arpeggios — running through the chromatic-triads-with-descending-chromatic-and-circle-of-5ths studies.

chapters.title: Chromatics / Triads / Arpeggios — Major and Minor Series, Circle of Fifths, Contrary Motion
chapters.notes: CHROMATICS—TRIADS—ARPEGGIOS MAJOR (line 11836, p.272) through the Major Series, Minor Series, 2nd-1st-Root and 1st-Root-2nd arpeggio orderings, circle-of-5ths chromatic triads, alternating-bass studies in two keys, repeating harmonic situations with various bass lines, and concludes with Chromatics CONTRARY MOTION (Major and Minor).

chapters.title: Chromatics — Triads — Blockouts: Explanations, Charts and Blockout Patterns
chapters.notes: CHROMATICS—TRIADS Blockouts section (line 12801, p.293) — Blockouts explanation, Blockout Charts (p.297), and the long sequence of Blockout Patterns at 3/4/6 keys per cycle x 12 cycles in repeat and mixed inversion orders, with ascending chromatic bass variations and Major/Minor mixed series. Runs to the end of the volume at p.334.
